text:
The greatest show on earth, theOlympic Games,bursts into life today with what is sure to be a memorable opening ceremony. In a break with tradition, the ceremony won’t take place in the Games’ athletics stadium, the Stade de France, but along a 6km stretch of the River Seine.

It will begin at Austerlitz bridge and end among the gardens, fountains and palaces in the shadow of the Eiffel Tower. It will feature 100 boats carrying more than 10,000 athletes, plus dignitaries, which will sail past Paris' iconic landmarks, including Notre Dame cathedral.

As well as transporting the athletes in the parade, the boats will also be used in the artistic part of the ceremony, which will showcase the history and culture of Paris and France. Other details, like the identity of performers, are being kept secret, but there are sure to be some surprise appearances.

Express Sportbrings you all the vital details about watching the ceremony, including start time and TV channel…

Festivities are due to commence at 7.30pm local time (6.30pm in the UK). It is expected to last around four hours with the final stages taking place as the sun sets across Paris. At the time of writing, the weather forecast looks mixed - with a risk of thunder showers in the evening.

The ceremony will be broadcast onBBCOne, with coverage getting underway at 5.45pm. The broadcast is due to conclude at 10pm but could well run over. You can also watch the ceremony via iPlayer and follow it on BBC Radio 5 Live.



Discovery, which holds the majority of UK TV rights for the Olympics, will broadcast the ceremony across its channels. You can watch on the Discovery app, TNT Sports 1 and Eurosport 1, with coverage beginning at 4.30pm UK time.

Discovery is currently running an Olympics offer, allowing fans to sign for £3.99 per month. Sky TV customers who get Eurosport 1 and Eurosport 2 can activate a Discovery+ standard plan at no extra cost.

In keeping with tradition, Greece will be the first nation to be introduced, with NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo one of their flag bearers. The Greeks will be followed by the Refugee Olympic Team, while hosts France will be the last to be introduced.

Athletes fromRussiaand Belarus competing as individuals because of the invasion ofUkrainewill not take part in the opening ceremony.

title: Olympics 2024 opening ceremony – TV, start time, order of events | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Mark Whiley
description: Express Sport brings you every you need to know about watching the opening ceremony for the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris.
